questions.question_text: Under the Subdivided Lands Disposition Act, when must a subdivision be registered with NDREC?
questions.correct_answer: Before any lots are sold in the state
distractors: Within 30 days after the first sale
After the developer sells 10 or more lots
Only when marketing to out-of-state buyers
questions.explanation: Under NDCC 43-23.1, subdivisions must be registered with NDREC BEFORE any lots are offered for sale in North Dakota. This applies to both in-state and out-of-state subdivisions.

questions.question_text: A Florida developer wants to sell subdivision lots to North Dakota residents. What must the developer do first?
distractors: Obtain a North Dakota broker license
File a plat with the county recorder
Get approval from the local zoning board
questions.explanation: Out-of-state subdivided land offered for sale to North Dakota residents must be registered with NDREC before any sales occur, just like in-state subdivisions.

questions.question_text: Which document is NOT typically required in a subdivision registration with NDREC?
distractors: A property description of the land
Details of financial arrangements
The developer's marketing plan
questions.explanation: Subdivision registration with NDREC requires a property description, financial arrangements, and marketing plan. A buyer's credit report is not part of the developer's registration filing.

questions.question_text: A developer creates a 50-lot rural subdivision in Burleigh County and begins selling lots without registering. What is the consequence?
distractors: The sales are valid but must be reported
Only a warning for the first offense
No penalty if buyers sign a waiver
questions.explanation: Selling unregistered subdivided land in North Dakota violates the Subdivided Lands Disposition Act and subjects the developer to penalties imposed by NDREC.

questions.question_text: Which North Dakota statute governs the registration of subdivided land sales?
distractors: NDCC 47-10
NDCC 32-19
NDCC 57-02
questions.explanation: The Subdivided Lands Disposition Act is codified under NDCC 43-23.1 and establishes registration requirements for subdivided land offered for sale in North Dakota.

questions.question_text: When must a buyer receive disclosure documents for a subdivided land purchase?
distractors: Within 10 days after signing
At the closing table only
Within 30 days of recording the deed
questions.explanation: Buyers of subdivided land in North Dakota must receive required disclosure documents before the purchase is completed, giving them the opportunity to review the information and make an informed decision.

questions.question_text: A buyer signs a contract for a subdivided lot but never received required disclosure documents. What remedy is available?
questions.correct_answer: The buyer may cancel the transaction
distractors: The buyer must proceed with the sale
The buyer can only renegotiate the price
The sale is automatically void after 90 days
questions.explanation: If a developer fails to provide required disclosure documents to the buyer, the buyer has cancellation rights as a consumer protection under North Dakota's subdivided land laws.

questions.question_text: A developer runs radio ads promoting a new subdivision. Which entity regulates the advertising content?
distractors: The Federal Trade Commission only
The county planning department
The North Dakota Attorney General
questions.explanation: Advertising of subdivided land in North Dakota must comply with NDREC rules. The commission has authority to regulate how subdivided land is marketed within the state.

questions.question_text: An out-of-state developer advertises "guaranteed investment returns" on subdivision lots in ND. What is the most likely outcome?
questions.correct_answer: NDREC enforcement action for false advertising
distractors: Approval if returns exceed 5% annually
No action unless a buyer complains
A fine only if the lots are unregistered
questions.explanation: Advertising subdivided land with guaranteed investment returns violates NDREC advertising rules. The commission can take enforcement action against misleading or false advertising of subdivided land.

questions.question_text: What is the primary purpose of North Dakota's subdivided land registration requirements?
distractors: Generating revenue for the state
Controlling urban sprawl in cities
Ensuring minimum lot sizes are met
questions.explanation: The Subdivided Lands Disposition Act's primary purpose is consumer protection, ensuring buyers receive adequate information and are protected from fraudulent or deceptive subdivided land sales.
